title: Module 1
content:
Links and Mechanisms: Definitions Link or Element, Kinematic Pairs,
Degrees of Freedom, Grubler's Criterion (without derivation), Kinematic
Chain, Mechanism, Structure, velocity and acceleration diagram of links in
mechanism (acceleration diagram description only), Inversion, Machine.
Static force analysis: Introduction: Static equilibrium. Equilibrium of two
and three force members. Members with two forces and torque, free body
diagrams, principle of virtual work. Force principle: D'Alembert's Principle,
Inertia force, inertia torque, dynamic equivalent system, Dynamic force
analysis of four-bar mechanism and slider crank mechanism (explanation
only).

title: Module 2
content:
Design of flat belt- material for belts, slip of the belts, creep, centrifugal
tension, Power transmitted by flat belts, Design procedure of flat belts.
Balancing of Rotating Masses: Static and dynamic balancing, balancing of
single rotating mass by balancing masses in same plane and in different
planes. Balancing of several rotating masses by balancing masses in same
plane and in different planes.
Cams: Types of Cams, Types of Followers. Displacement, Velocity &
Acceleration Time Curves for Cam Profiles. Disc Cam with Reciprocating
Follower Having Knife- Edge, Roller & Flat-Face Follower.

title: Module 3
content:
Disc Cam with Oscillating Roller Follower. Follower Motions including,
SHM, Uniform Velocity, Uniform Acceleration & Retardation and Cycloidal
Motion.
Introduction to the design process, Factors influencing machine design,
Selection
of
materials
based
on
mechanical
properties,
design
considerations, causes of failure in shafts, Direct, bending and torsional
stress equations. Design of solid and hollow shafts based on strength,
Rigidity and critical speed, material, design for static and fatigue loads,
repeated loading, reversed bending.

title: Module 4
content:
Design of helical, leaf, disc and torsional springs under constant loads.
Sliding contact bearing: - lubrication, lubricants, viscosity, journal bearings,
hydrodynamic theory, Petroff's equation, bearing characteristic number,
Sommerfeld number, Heat generated in bearings, Heat dissipated by
bearings, Design procedure of Journal bearings.
Ball and roller bearings: -
Types, bearing life, static and dynamic load capacity, Stribeck's Equation,
selection of bearings, selection of taper roller bearings, Design procedure of
Ball and roller bearings, Needle bearings.
